title: citharexylum fruticosum var. subserratum
intro: Citharexylum fruticosum var. subserratum, also known as Texas sage, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Texas sage can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can be dried and used in herbal remedies, or as a dried powder for topical applications.
